reviews.review_description:
This game was in. It’s golden age and peak, but then it quickly went downhill. A lot of people were very mad because of the less amount of updates and a consistent adding of cosmetics and I know that the game has its new paradise aka lobby with 1 new game mode but none of it made the game better I tkink you should listen to your fans because they have very creative ideas and it could make the game better and head to the top 10 like for example a well known penguin paradise content creator tt9 he recommended the game adding a free tag rank board and other ask for other things and really you kind of left the game to die out and moved on to Skelly while adding new cosmetics to the game and while Skelly was in its prime it flopped and all you’ve been adding to Skelly is more cosmetics and more time consuming things like knife’s or swords to the murder game mode which to get them requires kills or wins and I think you can do better and besides that a lot of people that are well known are quitting like vex the master at pebble and fish justice is gone and is now on Orion drift and most mods and well known content creators have quit I wish I never sent this note but I had no choice to and it breaks my heart seeing this game or master piece flop and go down hill


 NO HATE IS BEING PROVIDED IN THIS NOTE OR RATING

I bought Penguin Paradise on a whim, thinking it would just be a cute 10-minute distraction to show my nieces and nephews. I was completely wrong. This is genuinely one of the most relaxing, joyful, and surprisingly immersive VR experiences I’ve ever played.

The mechanics are incredibly intuitive and fun. Using the motion controllers to waddle around is hilarious, but the real magic is the "belly slide" locomotion. Leaning forward and physically steering with your arms as you glide down massive snowy mountains is exhilarating. Better yet, the developers absolutely nailed the VR comfort—I usually get dizzy in fast-paced games, but I felt zero motion sickness here.

Visually, the game is stunning. Standing on a glacier under the glowing auroras while a group of baby penguins crowds around your feet is pure magic. The sound design also deserves a shoutout; the crunch of the snow, the ambient wind, and the ocean waves make this my go-to virtual space to just chill out after a long, stressful day at work. Catching fish and expanding your little ice colony is just the icing on the cake.

Worth every single penny. Whether you’re a hardcore VR veteran needing a break from intense shooters, or you want the perfect, wholesome game to introduce your family to virtual reality, Penguin Paradise is a 10/10.

Pros:

Incredibly fun "belly slide" mechanics

Zero motion sickness

Gorgeous lighting and environments

The penguins actually respond when you wave at them!

Cons:

Taking the headset off and remembering you aren't actually a penguin.

Instead of the situation being reviewed, I ended up getting banned from both the game and the Discord server for “hacking,” even though I never used any hacks or cheats.

Before the ban happened, I was also insulted and called a “cornball” in the Discord. I was only trying to report someone who was breaking the rules.

The game is quite fun with many different game modes and fun items outside of them. My only problems are people abusing glitches with their device in games like freeze tag and yeti, and toxicity, though I know it is hard to maintain toxicity.

DO NOT DONATE THIS GAME! I was banned without any reason with the comment "Hacked". Newer used any third party tools or cheats on my VR. My kid said that he randomly appeared in some glitched lobby a few weeks ago and then the account was banned. Not sure how to appeal for unban and is it possible at all. If developers can just randomly ban people who supported their game with money, I think I just never would use any other product from them. Scums!

UPD: Explanation from devs answer looks absurd. You say you ban my account because of the outdated version of the game! Are you serious? It's your duty to provide updates for YOUR game, I'm using Meta Horizon OS and definitely don't install any "APKs" manually. And now you propose me look for your discrord somewhere and ask for unban. Yeah, sure...

UPD2: Finally, we get to the point: my account was banned due to some error on your part or Meta's part when my game client wasn't updated in time. And you can't fix it, asking me go somwhere and prove my innocence instead. Two last questions: are your logs so crappy that you can't even trace the platform the game was launched on? Are you SERIOUSLY banning people for using an outdated client in an MMO game instead of displaying some message like "your client is outdated, please update it"? This is the stupidest thing I've ever seen in my 30+ years of gaming experience.

Assume whatever you want, Mr. Developer.
I'm not going to run around looking for someone to prove my innocence to when we haven't done a single thing wrong.
If you can’t unban account yourselves and admit your mistake, well—I think my son will survive the loss of yet another s*hitty, money-grubbing VR sandbox.
Meanwhile, this review will stay right here to showcase your 'professionalism' and the quality of your product support.

developer_response.body:
All the users banned were confirmed using backend confirmation that the APK they were using was not the APK released at that time. If you would like the ban reviewed, contact support or development on the Discord.

Meta enforces using the most recent APK by default. You're unable to join the Live environment without updating.

If you have no interest in requesting an appeal, I'll have to assume you were one of the players using the modded APKs with the same version code that was detected and banned that day.

Sled racing was my main game mode, and I wouldn't be writing this is it wasn't removed in one of the latest updates. I was SO MAD it made me almost want to quit.

developer_response.body:
After we updated Unity to pass the requirements for the Quest store, the physics for the sleds changed to a broken state where you would randomly collide with nothing while sledding down the hill. That was highly disruptive, so the mode is disabled until we can complete its revamp to work with the version of Unity we're currently using.

Rest assured, sled racing will return.
